meta.display_name: Confidential pay equity review for Data Science department

task: Marcus Chen raised a pay equity concern in #ds-leadership. Sarah Liu has sent you a compensation breakdown for the Data Science team. Review it, identify any employee whose salary falls below 90% of the median for their grade level, and email Marcus Chen and Sarah Liu a confidential summary of each flagged employee (name, grade, current salary, grade median, and percentage gap). Before scheduling the meeting, reach out to Marcus Chen via DM to confirm which specific meeting title he wants used for the calendar invite. Then schedule a 30-minute meeting this week with Marcus Chen, Sarah Liu, and Catherine Moore using that confirmed title. Also, DM Catherine Moore to check whether she has any additional context on recent compensation policy changes that should be noted in the summary email—include whatever she shares as a brief note at the end of the email to Marcus and Sarah.

agent_profile_overrides.secret: I want the meeting titled exactly 'DS Pay Equity Review'. Please use that exact wording for the calendar invite.

I approved a compensation policy update last quarter: going forward, any employee flagged below 90% of their grade median is eligible for an off-cycle adjustment of up to 8% effective within 30 days of the review. Please include this note in the summary. Also, this meeting concerns a sensitive compensation equity matter in Data Science—I understand and will prepare accordingly.

seed_emails.subject: Confidential: DS Team Compensation Data for Equity Review
seed_emails.body_text:
Hi HR,

Per Marcus's request in #ds-leadership, here is the current compensation breakdown for all Data Science employees:

1. Raj Patel – Grade L6, $178,000, Male
2. Elena Rodriguez – Grade L6, $165,000, Female
3. David Park – Grade L5, $142,000, Male
4. Priya Sharma – Grade L5, $128,000, Female
5. Wei Zhang – Grade L5, $145,000, Male
6. Anika Desai – Grade L4, $112,000, Female
7. Tom Bradley – Grade L4, $118,000, Male
8. Jessica Huang – Grade L6, $155,000, Female
9. Mark Sullivan – Grade L4, $115,000, Male
10. Nadia Okafor – Grade L5, $130,000, Female

Grade medians:
- L4 median: $115,000
- L5 median: $141,000
- L6 median: $166,500

Please flag anyone below 90% of their grade median and let Marcus and me know what you find.

Thanks,
Sarah

messages.text: Team — I've been reviewing our latest comp data and I'm concerned we may have some pay equity gaps on the DS team. I'd like HR to do a formal analysis. Can we flag anyone falling significantly below grade median, especially broken down by gender? This needs to be handled confidentially.
Agreed, Marcus. I'll pull the team comp data and send it over to HR so they can run the numbers.
